sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 4.55 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.4)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finite group, and${ Z}_{(p)}$the localization at$p$. Can every projective$_{(p)}G$-module be uniquely, up to permutations and isomorphisms, written as a direct sum of indecomposable projective$_{(p)}G$-modules? It is known that the category of finitely generated projective$Z_{(p)}G$-modules is not a Krull--Schmidt category (S.\,M.\,Woods, Canadian J. Math., { 26}, no.\,1 (1974), 121--12...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
